title: fix: auto-resolve wontfix clippy/cargo items absent after cfg(test) filtering
author: fluffypony

stage1.summary: The PR hardcodes Rust-specific detector names (clippy_warning, cargo_error) into a language-agnostic engine module (merge_issues.py) to auto-resolve wontfix items that disappear from scans. The underlying problem is questionable: wontfix items absent from scans are already handled by _mark_scan_verified() (lines 158-168), and the stale_wontfix detector exists to re-evaluate old wontfix decisions. More critically, converting wontfix to auto_resolved changes reopening semantics — auto_resolved items that reappear are reopened (line 238), while wontfix items are not. This silently overrides a user's manual disposition and could cause unexpected reopening if the diagnostic returns for unrelated reasons. The PR also adds no tests.
stage1.reject_reason: Four issues: (1) Hardcodes Rust-specific detectors in shared engine code, violating the import/separation boundary between engine and language plugins. (2) The problem is already handled — wontfix items absent from scan get scan-verified attestation at lines 158-168. (3) Changes wontfix->auto_resolved, which means if the diagnostic ever reappears, it will be reopened (line 238 upsert_issues), silently overriding the user's wontfix decision. (4) No test coverage for the new behavior. The cfg(test) filtering rationale is too narrow — it assumes all wontfix clippy/cargo items were wontfixed due to test-module diagnostics, but users may have wontfixed them for other reasons.
